items.headline: Historic house preserved while adding four townhomes at Crescent Avenue
items.summary: A Heritage Preservation Commission hearing will decide whether to allow four three-story townhomes to be built behind a 960-square-foot historic house at 434 Crescent Avenue, balancing new density with preservation of an existing historic resource.

items.headline: City Council Approves New Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel
items.summary: The council amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC regarding large format digital signage on city-owned property. This could affect where and how digital advertisements appear throughout San José.

items.headline: City Issues Request for Encampment Cleanup Services
items.summary: The council reviewed proposals for contracting encampment cleanup services, a significant operational change in how the city will handle unhoused encampments. This represents a policy shift in the city's approach to homelessness management.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project Moves Forward
items.summary: The council took action on the Terminal Elevator Replacement Project. See the linked agenda for project location, scope, and timeline.

items.headline: City negotiating purchase of Finch Avenue property with school district
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations to acquire or transact real estate at 10480 Finch Avenue, working with the Cupertino Union School District on price and payment terms. The outcome could affect future land use in this area.

items.headline: Milpitas Adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, which will guide future green space and recreation development in this key area. This plan shapes what parks and outdoor amenities residents will have access to as the Innovation District continues to develop.

items.headline: Two large apartment complexes approved at El Paseo De Saratoga
items.summary: El Paseo De Saratoga (the West San José shopping center on Saratoga Avenue) will add 772 residential units across two buildings totaling $125.7 million in construction. The project significantly expands housing supply in this part of San José.

items.headline: 260-unit affordable housing complex approved in Berryessa
items.summary: Berryessa Family Apartments will bring 260 new units to 1 Bernard Dr with $52.4 million investment. The project addresses critical affordable housing needs in the North San José area.
